questions.original_question: 路特斯科技截止3季度的全球交付数量是多少？增长如何？
questions.answer: 截至2024年第三季度，路特斯科技的全球交付量达到了7,617辆，同比增长136%。这一强劲增长主要得益于公司在全球市场的持续扩展和产品线的丰富，特别是在欧洲和新兴市场的表现尤为突出。其中，欧洲市场的交付量同比增长了372%，其他地区的增长也达到了110%。此外，路特斯科技在2024年第三季度成功推出了多款新车型，进一步推动了交付量的增长。以上信息基于2025年1月15日之前的知识。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – January 14, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

rag_info.chunk_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

rag_info.chunk_content: "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering pursuit of performance and excellence, the company made steady progress with continuing operational growth in the first half of 2024," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "In the first half of 2024, the deliveries soared to nearly 4,900 vehicles, up 239% year-on-year. Lotus Technology achieved an even more balanced distribution of deliveries in all key markets globally. The US market recorded extraordinary growth after Lotus Technology’s re-entry into the market, contributing to 26% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology launched the 'Win26' plan to increase resilience, strengthen the brand, and strive for sustainable growth. Going forward, Lotus Technology will keep executing its plans, boosting efficiencies and competitiveness, and remain committed to its customers, investors, and stakeholders." "Lotus Technology continued delivery of strong topline growth in the first half of 2024, with total revenues and gross profit up by 206% and 731% year-over-year respectively,” said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer. "Gross margin for the first half of 2024 increased by 8 percentage points year-over-year but shrank 2 percentage points compared to year end of 2023 as a result of increased cost of the Emira sportscar model due to inflation and seasonal variation in product sales mix. Lotus Technology's average selling price (ASP) remained above \$100,000 in the first half of 2024 despite intensified competition. The execution of the 'Win26' plan will enable Lotus Technology to achieve positive EBITDA and operating cash flow in 2026, to boost financial performance, strengthen the balance sheet, and deliver commitments to shareholders.”

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Our Business**

Lotus is a premier global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, renowned for designing and developing luxury lifestyle vehicles under its iconic British brand. Established in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has a rich heritage of over seven decades in racing, symbolizing excellence in performance, design, and engineering, with a legacy that includes 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles.

In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK, transforming the company into a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer. This strategic partnership enhances Lotus's capabilities in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), and logistics, facilitating a transition from a traditional sports car company to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles. The luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only about 10 existing models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine luxury models, positioning Lotus to capitalize on first-mover advantages.

Lotus launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022, with deliveries commencing in 2023. The Eletre features an advanced 800-volt EPA platform, offering three versions: Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R. The Eletre R achieves an impressive 905 horsepower and accelerates from 0-100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery provides a WLTP range of 490 km and can be charged from 10% to 80% in under 20 minutes, with an average MSRP exceeding $100,000. Following the Eletre, the second model, the Emeya, was launched in September 2023, with deliveries expected to begin in March 2024.

Lotus's R&D capabilities are a competitive strength, highlighted by the proprietary 800-volt EPA developed over five years, which supports rapid model rollout and production scalability. The company also features advanced ADAS technology, including the first deployable LiDAR system. R&D facilities in Europe and China foster collaboration among experienced teams, enhancing innovation.

All BEV lifestyle models are manufactured through a contract partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This partnership enhances scalability and flexibility while minimizing upfront capital commitments. Access to Geely’s global supply chain allows Lotus to secure high-quality components at competitive prices, effectively managing supply chain risks.

Lotus's sales strategy emphasizes a digital-first, omni-channel approach, providing a luxury retail experience through premium stores and a comprehensive digital platform. As of June 30, 2024, the company has established a global distribution network with 207 stores and a Global Commercial Platform for distributing Lotus vehicles, including the Eletre and Emeya. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, with 4,873 vehicles delivered in the first half of 2024, encompassing both lifestyle and sports cars.

This comprehensive approach positions Lotus as a leader in the luxury BEV market, leveraging its rich heritage, innovative technology, and strategic partnerships to drive future growth.

- **Table Title:** Iconic Brand – Historical Achievements of Lotus Technology Inc.
- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.
- **Context:** The context emphasizes Lotus Technology Inc.’s legacy as a leader in motorsport innovation and luxury category recognition, underlining its transformation and status as a globally recognized brand.
- **Special Notes:** All years are in chronological order, and specific achievements are tied to models or events. The laurels summarize Lotus Technology Inc.'s accomplishments in motorsport (e.g., F1 Constructors’ World Championships) and Le Mans victories.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024 on October 17, 2024, reporting a total of 7,543 vehicles delivered, which represents a remarkable 134% increase compared to the same period last year. The deliveries were well-distributed across global markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World each contributing approximately 20-35% to the overall delivery figures.

In the third quarter of 2024, the company successfully delivered the Emeya (GT, Sedan) in Europe, while the Eletre (SUV) expanded its reach to markets including the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), Japan, and Korea. Notably, the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant specifically designed for the North American market, was revealed in September 2024, with a starting price set at $229,900. Deliveries for this model are anticipated to commence in the first half of 2025. Additionally, bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre and Emeya began their deliveries in Q3 2024.

Lotus also showcased the Theory 1 concept car in September, highlighting its commitment to advanced technology and eco-friendly features. It is important to note that the delivery figures provided are based on reports from business units and may differ from those recorded in financial accounting systems. This comprehensive overview underscores Lotus Technology's significant growth trajectory and its strategic expansion into various international markets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Technology" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where the Company operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

title: Operating Highlights for the First Half of
2024 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the Company achieved a remarkable 239% year-on-year increase in total deliveries, reaching 4,873 units, with significant contributions from various global regions, each accounting for 20-30% of the total. The US market notably represented 26% of these deliveries, reflecting the Company's successful re-entry into the market. 

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans totaling 2,389 units (up 174% from 2023) and Sportscars reaching 2,484 units (a 337% increase). Regionally, Europe led with 1,459 deliveries (30% of total), followed by North America with 1,278 (26%), China with 1,208 (25%), and the Rest of the World with 928 (19%).

The Company is evolving from a traditional sports car manufacturer to an intelligent luxury mobility provider, focusing on technology-related revenue through intelligent driving solutions and R&D services. The introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service caters to growing customer demand for personalized designs, enhancing the brand's luxury appeal and potentially improving long-term gross margins.

In light of changing market conditions and uncertainties related to new tariff policies, the Company has revised its 2024 delivery target to 12,000 units. Additionally, the "Win26" plan has been launched to achieve positive EBITDA by 2026, emphasizing process optimization, cost-saving measures, and product strategy recalibration to better meet the needs of diverse global markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering pursuit of performance and excellence, the company made steady progress with continuing operational growth in the first half of 2024," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. Lotus Technology launched the 'Win26' plan to increase resilience, strengthen the brand, and strive for sustainable growth. Going forward, Lotus Technology will keep executing its plans, boosting efficiencies and competitiveness, and remain committed to its customers, investors, and stakeholders." "Gross margin for the first half of 2024 increased by 8 percentage points year-over-year but shrank 2 percentage points compared to year end of 2023 as a result of increased cost of the Emira sportscar model due to inflation and seasonal variation in product sales mix. Lotus Technology's average selling price (ASP) remained above \$100,000 in the first half of 2024 despite intensified competition. The execution of the 'Win26' plan will enable Lotus Technology to achieve positive EBITDA and operating cash flow in 2026, to boost financial performance, strengthen the balance sheet, and deliver commitments to shareholders.”

title: CEO and CFO comments 
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus achieved remarkable operational growth, with vehicle deliveries soaring to nearly 4,900, marking a 239% increase from the previous year. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

title: Operating and Financial Results for the Third Quarter of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating and Financial Results for Q3 2024**

In the third quarter of 2024, the company experienced a notable increase in total deliveries, reporting 2,744 units, which represents a significant 54% year-over-year growth. This surge in deliveries contributed to total revenues of $255 million, reflecting a 36% increase from $188 million in the same quarter of the previous year. Despite the positive trends in deliveries and revenues, the company faced considerable challenges in profitability, as evidenced by a sharp decline in gross margin to 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. Consequently, gross profit fell to $8 million, marking a substantial 70% decrease from $28 million in the prior year.

The operating loss for Q3 2024 was reported at $160 million, indicating a slight improvement of 2% compared to the $163 million loss recorded in Q3 2023. However, the net loss increased to $206 million, which is a 19% rise from the $174 million net loss reported in the same quarter last year. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) worsened, showing a loss of $182 million, an 18% increase from the $154 million loss in Q3 2023.

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans accounting for 1,594 units delivered, reflecting a robust 79% year-over-year increase, while Sportscars saw deliveries of 1,150 units, up 30% from the previous year. Overall, the financial results for Q3 2024 highlight a challenging operational environment, characterized by significant losses despite the increase in both deliveries and revenues. All financial data presented are unaudited and expressed in millions of U.S. dollars.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,194 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where Lotus Technology operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Operating and Financial Results for the Third Quarter of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating and Financial Results for Q3 2024**

In the third quarter of 2024, the company experienced a notable increase in total deliveries, reporting 2,744 units, which represents a significant 54% year-over-year growth. This surge in deliveries contributed to total revenues of $255 million, reflecting a 36% increase from $188 million in the same quarter of the previous year. Despite the positive trends in deliveries and revenues, the company faced considerable challenges in profitability, as evidenced by a sharp decline in gross margin to 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. Consequently, gross profit fell to $8 million, marking a substantial 70% decrease from $28 million in the prior year.

The operating loss for Q3 2024 was reported at $160 million, indicating a slight improvement of 2% compared to the $163 million loss recorded in Q3 2023. However, the net loss increased to $206 million, which is a 19% rise from the $174 million net loss reported in the same quarter last year. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) worsened, showing a loss of $182 million, an 18% increase from the $154 million loss in Q3 2023.

Deliveries were categorized by model type, with Lifestyle SUVs and Sedans accounting for 1,594 units delivered, reflecting a robust 79% year-over-year increase, while Sportscars saw deliveries of 1,150 units, up 30% from the previous year. Overall, the financial results for Q3 2024 highlight a challenging operational environment, characterized by significant losses despite the increase in both deliveries and revenues. All financial data presented are unaudited and expressed in millions of U.S. dollars.

title: Operational and Financial Summaries 
summary: The operational results for Lotus Tech for the first quarter of 2024 (1Q 2024) reveal substantial growth across various performance metrics compared to the same period in 2023 (1Q 2023). Key highlights include a dramatic increase in vehicle deliveries, with total units reaching 2,194, marking a 731% rise from 264 units in 1Q 2023. However, the cost of revenues also escalated to 143 million, a 653% increase, leading to a gross profit of 30 million and a gross margin of 18%. Despite these positive trends, the company faced challenges with an operating loss of 233 million, a 44% increase from the prior year, and a net loss of 258 million, up 61%. Adjusted EBITDA also declined to -204 million.

Overall, while Lotus Tech has demonstrated significant growth in deliveries and revenue, it continues to experience substantial operating and net losses as it advances its Vision80 plan for international expansion and innovation. The performance indicators underscore both the potential and the challenges the organization faces in a competitive market.
